short: Ayo Owolabi is a builder making software that lets one person operate like a whole team.
medium: Ayo Owolabi builds software that solves real problems for the people who run things, mostly the scattered back-office work that quietly keeps a business going. His products give independent operators the kind of leverage that used to require a team. He writes about building, the craft of doing it largely on your own, and the shifts in technology that go deeper than the usual takes.
long:
Ayo Owolabi is a builder based in Newcastle upon Tyne. He makes software that solves real, unglamorous problems, the kind of work that quietly keeps a business running. He runs a portfolio of products at different stages. Scarab HQ is an AI-augmented back office that brings every channel a founder works across into one place, so one person can operate like a team. Minipod is the calm back office for coaches. Legworker takes the legwork out of the job search. Intentlift turns visitor intent into revenue for businesses. Through his consultancy, Bedrock, he does hands-on product and engineering work for teams that need to ship something real.

He writes about building, the craft of making software largely on your own, and the shifts in technology that go deeper than the usual takes.

He is based in Newcastle upon Tyne.
